title: Arguments and Parameters 📩
description: Make your methods flexible by feeding them outside data.

slides.title: Flexible Methods 🤸
slides.content:
A method that prints 'Hello World' every time is a bit rigid.

What if we want a method that can say hello to *any* name we give it?

We can do this by using **Parameters** and **Arguments** to pass data into the method.

slides.title: Defining a Parameter
slides.content:
A parameter is a temporary variable that acts like a slot waiting to be filled.

You place parameters in parentheses `()` right after the method name.

```ruby
def greet(name)
  puts "Hello there, #{name}!"
end
```

slides.title: Passing Arguments
slides.content: When you CALL the method, you pass in the actual data (the "Argument"). This real data fills the parameter slot.
slides.codeSnippet:
def greet(name)
  puts "Hello there, #{name}!"
end

greet("Alice")
greet("Bob")

quizData.question: In the code `greet("Alice")`, what is "Alice"?
quizData.explanation: The placeholder waiting for data (`name`) is the parameter. The actual 100% real data you feed into it (`"Alice"`) is the argument.

slides.title: Multiple Parameters 👯
slides.content:
You can define as many parameters as you want by separating them with commas.

```ruby
def create_user(first_name, last_name, age)
  puts "Welcome #{first_name} #{last_name}! Age: #{age}"
end
```

You must provide exactly the right amount of arguments when calling it, in the exact same order!

slides.title: Passing Multiple
slides.content: Ruby will carefully align the arguments based on the order you passed them.
slides.codeSnippet:
def add_numbers(num1, num2)
  puts num1 + num2
end

add_numbers(10, 5)

quizData.question: If a method is defined with two parameters, what happens if you try to call it with only one argument?

options.text: Ruby replaces the second parameter with nil.
Ruby throws an ArgumentError and crashes.
It skips the code requiring the second parameter.

quizData.explanation: If a method demands two pieces of data to work, providing only one will trigger an immediate `ArgumentError: wrong number of arguments (given 1, expected 2)`.
